basic_details.job_title: GSSSB Female Health Worker Class 3 Recruitment 2026 – 155 Posts, Apply Online by 29 June
basic_details.short_information: Gujarat Gaun Seva Pasandgi Mandal (GSSSB) has released Advertisement No. 416/202627 for the recruitment of 155 Female Health Worker (Class-3) posts under the Health and Family Welfare Department (Urban Health/UDHAV municipalities). Online applications open on 15 June 2026 and close on 29 June 2026 (23:59 hrs) at ojas.gujarat.gov.in. Only female candidates are eligible to apply.

application_fee.details: The examination fee must be paid online only through the OJAS portal. Candidates who appear for the exam will receive a refund of the fee through the same payment mode used. Candidates who do not appear for the exam will not receive any refund. | Fee payment last date: 01 July 2026 (23:59 hrs). Payment accepted via Debit Card, Internet Banking, UPI, or Wallets. Transaction charges apply additionally. Applications without fee confirmation will be rejected without notice.

qualification.details: Eligibility for this recruitment is defined by both educational qualification and age limit. Only female candidates holding a recognised nursing or health worker training certificate may apply. Candidates must also possess basic computer knowledge as prescribed under Gujarat Civil Services Rules. | Passed Secondary (SSC) or Higher Secondary (HSC) examination from a recognised board, AND Completed the Female Health Workers Basic Training Course recognised by the Government, OR completed the Auxiliary Nurse Midwife (ANM) Course recognised by the Government and registered with the Gujarat Nursing Council Basic knowledge of computer applications as per Gujarat Civil Services Classification and Recruitment (General) Rules, 1967 Adequate knowledge of Gujarati or Hindi or both | Computer Knowledge Note: Candidates who do not currently hold a computer certificate may still apply, but must submit a valid Basic Computer Knowledge certificate before receiving an appointment order. Failure to do so will result in cancellation of selection. | Since all 155 posts are designated for female candidates only, the minimum age is 18 years and the upper age is calculated as on 29 June 2026. All reserved category benefits are available only to candidates originally domiciled in Gujarat. | The following disability types are eligible for this post: Low Vision (LV, 40–70%), Deaf (D), Hard of Hearing (HH), One Leg (OL), Cerebral Palsy (CP), Leprosy Cured (LC), Dwarfism (Dw), Acid Attack Victim (AAV), Spinal Deformity (SD), Spinal Injury (SI), Intellectual Disability (ID), Specific Learning Disability (SLD), Mental Illness (MI), and Multiple Disabilities. BA (Both Arms) and BL (Both Legs) types are subject to Medical Board opinion. | DON'T MISSSSC CGL 12256 Group B & Group C Online Form 202612,256 Posts • Group B & Group C • SSC22 Jun 202612,256 postsRRB 6565 Technician Online Form 2026 (Short Notice)6,565 Vacancies • Technician29 Jul 20266,565 postsOSSSC 5989 Nursing Officer Online Form 20265,989 Openings • OSSSC13 Jul 20265,989 postsGSRTC 4599 Driver Online Form 20264,599 Posts • GSRTC05 Jul 20264,599 postsCentral Bank of India 4,500 Apprentice Recruitment 20264,500 Vacancies • Apprentice • Central Bank of India22 Jun 20264,500 postsGSRTC 4318 Conductor Online Form 20264,318 Openings • Conductor05 Jul 20264,318 posts

salary_details.details: Selected candidates will initially be appointed on a fixed pay basis for the first five years. After satisfactory completion of five years of service, they become eligible for regularisation in the 7th Pay Commission pay scale. | Regularisation after five years is subject to satisfactory performance and the outcome of SLP No. 14124/2012 and SLP No. 14125/2012 pending before the Hon'ble Supreme Court. Other admissible allowances will be as per relevant Government resolutions.

selection_process: Selection will be through a single-stage competitive written examination. There is no interview stage. The exam is conducted in MCQ (Multiple Choice Question) format using either CBRT (Computer Based Response Test) or OMR method.
Written Examination (MCQ/CBRT or OMR) – Single stage

3 hours Provisional Result Publication – On gsssb.gujarat.gov.in after exam Provisional Answer Key – Published after exam; objections accepted online Final Answer Key – Published after considering valid objections Certificate Verification – ~2× of available posts shortlisted on merit Selection List – Final merit-based selection list published Appointment – By concerned department of the State Government
Minimum qualifying marks: 40% in Part-A and 40% in Part-B - applicable for all categories. Negative marking of 1/4 mark applies for each wrong answer.
Widow candidates: 5% bonus marks added to final MCQ/CBRT score (subject to not having remarried) Sports persons: 5% bonus marks for candidates who have represented at National/International
or All India School Federation level competitions in recognised sports

pattern_table.Part: Constitution of India, Current Affairs, Gujarati & English Comprehension

exam_pattern.details: The competitive exam is divided into two parts in a single question paper. Part-A tests general aptitude and Part-B tests domain-specific nursing knowledge. The total duration is 3 hours (180 minutes).

syllabus.details: The syllabus is divided into Part-A (general aptitude) and Part-B (nursing domain knowledge). Part-A questions will be in both Gujarati and English. Part-B questions will also be bilingual (Gujarati and English). | 1. Reasoning & Data Interpretation (30 marks): Problems on Ages, Venn Diagrams, Visual Reasoning, Blood Relations, Arithmetic Reasoning, Data Interpretation (Charts, Graphs, Tables), Data Sufficiency. | 2. Quantitative Aptitude (30 marks): Number Systems, Simplification & Algebra, AP & GP, Average, Percentage, Profit-Loss, Ratio & Proportion, Partnership, Time & Work, Time-Speed-Distance, Work-Wages & Chain Rule. | 3. Constitution of India (10 marks): Preamble, Fundamental Rights, DPSP, Fundamental Duties, Powers of President/Vice President/Governor, Parliamentary System, Constitutional Amendments, Emergency Provisions, Centre-State Relations, Judicial System, Constitutional Bodies. | 4. Current Affairs (10 marks): State, national and international events of importance. | 5. Comprehension (10 marks): Gujarati comprehension (5 marks) + English comprehension (5 marks) - paragraph-based or statement/assertion type questions.

physical_eligibility.details: With 155 posts spread across Gujarat's municipal health sector and open to all eligible female candidates statewide, competition is expected to be high. The SEBC category has the highest allocation (80 posts), while the ST category accounts for 43 posts. General category candidates compete for only 17 posts. The single-stage exam structure with negative marking means accuracy matters as much as speed. The Part-B nursing domain section (120 marks) carries significantly more weight than Part-A (90 marks), making technical preparation the primary differentiator.

how_to_apply: Applications must be submitted online only at ojas.gujarat.gov.in. No offline or postal applications will be accepted. Candidates should keep all original certificates ready before filling in the form, as no corrections are allowed after confirmation.
Click on Online Application → Apply → Select GSSSB
Click on the advertisement for Advertisement No. 416/202627 – Female Health Worker Class-3
Click Apply Now, then Skip to open the application form
Fill in Personal Details (fields marked * are mandatory)
Accept the Assurance/Declaration and click Save - your Application Number will be generated
Upload Photograph (5 cm × 3.6 cm, JPG, max 15 KB, white background with date) and Signature (2.5 cm × 7.5 cm, JPG, max 15 KB)
Click Confirm Application and verify all details - a Confirmation Number will be generated (save this carefully)
Click Print Application and download a copy for your records
Pay the exam fee online by 01 July 2026 via Debit Card / Internet Banking / UPI / Wallet
Save the fee payment receipt - without it, the application is invalid

Important: Once the application is confirmed, no changes can be made by either the candidate or the Board. If a correction is needed, submit a fresh application before the last date and pay the fee again. If multiple applications are submitted, the last confirmed application with fee will be treated as valid.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for GSSSB Female Health Worker Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application is 29 June 2026 (11:59 PM). The last date to pay the exam fee is 01 July 2026 (23:59 hrs).

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are available in GSSSB Female Health Worker Class-3 2026?
faq.answer: A total of 155 posts are available - 17 General, 15 EWS, 80 SEBC, and 43 ST. SC posts are nil in this notification. Divyang posts will be notified separately.

faq.question: Q3. Can male candidates apply for this recruitment?
faq.answer: No. These posts are exclusively reserved for female candidates only. Male candidates are not eligible to apply.

faq.question: Q4. What is the educational qualification required?
faq.answer: Candidates must have passed SSC or HSC and completed the Female Health Workers Basic Training Course, or the ANM Course and be registered with Gujarat Nursing Council. Basic computer knowledge is also required.

faq.question: Q5. What is the age limit for GSSSB Female Health Worker 2026?
faq.answer: The general upper age limit is 40 years as on 29 June 2026. Female candidates from General category get a 5-year relaxation (max 45 years), and reserved category females get 10-year relaxation (max 45 years).

faq.question: Q6. What is the exam fee for this recruitment?
faq.answer: The exam fee is ₹500 for General category and ₹400 for reserved category candidates (including all female, SEBC, SC, ST, EWS, Divyang, and Ex-Servicemen). Fee is refundable if the candidate appears for the exam.

faq.question: Q7. What is the selection process for GSSSB Female Health Worker Class-3?
faq.answer: Selection is through a single-stage MCQ-based written exam (CBRT or OMR), totalling 210 questions and 210 marks in 3 hours, followed by certificate verification and merit-based selection list preparation.

faq.question: Q8. What is the salary for Female Health Worker Class-3 in Gujarat?
faq.answer: Selected candidates receive a fixed pay of ₹26,000/- per month for the first five years. On satisfactory completion of five years, they are eligible for regularisation in Pay Level-2 (₹19,900 to ₹63,200) under the 7th Pay Commission.

faq.question: Q9. Is there negative marking in the exam?
faq.answer: Yes. 1/4 mark is deducted for every wrong answer. Unattempted questions carry no penalty.

faq.question: Q10. Where can I apply and where is the official website?
faq.answer: Applications must be submitted online at ojas.gujarat.gov.in. The official recruitment notification is available at gsssb.gujarat.gov.in.

faq.question: What is the last date to apply for GSSSB Female Health Worker Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application is 29 June 2026 (11:59 PM). The last date to pay the exam fee is 01 July 2026 (23:59 hrs).
